brand.description: Buy and sell Gir and Girolando dairy genetics for the tropics — semen and embryos exported to Africa, Southeast Asia, and Latin America. Explore the breed's journey from the Gir forest of India to Brazil's dairy revolution, foundation bloodlines, record milk cows, and translated global news. Free to list.

products.label: Semen
products.blurb: Export-ready straws from proven Gir & Girolando dairy sires.

products.label: Embryos
products.blurb: IVF & in-vivo embryos — a complete tropical-dairy genetic package.

registry_names: ABCZ (Associação Brasileira dos Criadores de Zebu)
vocab.help_nuance: Gir (Gyr) is an ancient Bos indicus DAIRY zebu from the Gir forest of Gujarat, India — domed forehead, long pendulous ears, and exceptional heat, humidity, and tick tolerance. In Brazil it was transformed into a high-yielding tropical dairy breed and is the foundation of Girolando (Gir × Holstein). This marketplace is scoped to the EXPORT window — Brazilian and Indian dairy genetics flowing to Africa, Southeast Asia, and Latin America — and does not try to replace Brazil's domestic platforms. Export always depends on the importing country's health protocol (FMD status, bluetongue, TB/brucellosis testing), so eligibility is disclosed per listing, not assumed.

copy.heroTitle: Gir & Girolando dairy genetics — built for the tropics

copy.roundupTitle: Every exportable Gir genetics listing, one feed
copy.roundupIntro: The Roundup brings the world's Gir and Girolando dairy-genetics market together: listings posted on GirTank plus semen and embryo offerings we track from breeders' and exporters' public pages (marked 📡 — those link straight back to the original seller's page; web sellers pay nothing and can remove a listing anytime).
copy.theaterIntro: The world's Gir and Girolando video library — dairy sires on film, milking records, expo footage, and farm tours. Searchable by registration number.
copy.foundationIntro: The dairy Gir of the tropics traces from the sacred cattle of India's Gir forest through the Brazilian herds that turned them into a milk breed. Meet the animals behind the genetics.
